target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.
target.author: stevenepalmer

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(daemon): escape systemd cgroup for update handoff via systemd-run --scope
target.body:
## Summary

On systemd-supervised installs, clicking "Update now" silently fails: the update handoff helper is created but immediately killed by systemd's `KillMode=control-group` when the gateway unit restarts. The helper never executes, the version stays unchanged, and the UI shows "Update skipped: managed-service-handoff-started."

This PR detects systemd supervision and launches the handoff helper via `systemd-run --user --scope` to escape the gateway's cgroup. Non-systemd supervisors (launchd, etc.) continue using the existing detached-child path.

## Root cause

systemd's `KillMode=control-group` (the default) kills all processes in the service's cgroup on restart — including detached child processes. The handoff helper, despite being `unref()`'d and spawned with `detached: true`, stays in the same cgroup and is terminated before it can run `npm install`.

## Fix

```
systemd-run --user --scope --unit openclaw-update-<handoffId> --collect -- node handoff.cjs handoff.json
```

Key design choices:

**`--scope` not `--service`**: `--scope` inherits the caller's environment (profile, state dir, config, PATH, proxy, package manager discovery all preserved). `--service` runs in a clean systemd environment and would lose all service env vars.

**`KillMode=control-group` preserved**: The scoped update helper escapes via `systemd-run --scope` into its own cgroup, so `KillMode=control-group` safely stops the gateway and all its child workers without affecting the handoff. No change to existing shutdown semantics.

**Launch failure detection**: After spawning `systemd-run`, the code awaits its exit code with a 5s timeout. If systemd-run exits non-zero (user session unavailable, D-Bus down, etc.), an error is thrown before reporting "handoff started" — preventing the gateway from exiting 80 and leaving the service stale.

**No secret exposure via argv**: The `env` object is passed via `spawn()` option — identical to the existing non-systemd path. No `--setenv=KEY=*** args are used. Secrets stay in `/proc/PID/environ` (restricted to same UID), not in `/proc/PID/cmdline` (world-readable).

Additional hardening:
- `sanitizeSystemdUnitName()` strips non-`[a-zA-Z0-9:._-]` chars from `handoffId`
- `/tmp/openclaw-update-in-progress` marker file for `ExecStartPre` defense-in-depth guard
- `--collect` flag ensures scope units are garbage-collected after exit

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `update-managed-service-handoff.ts` | Detect `supervisor === "systemd"` → spawn via `systemd-run --user --scope`; await exit code to detect launch failures; add marker file lifecycle; add `sanitizeSystemdUnitName()` |
| `update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts` | 3 new tests: systemd `--scope` spawn path with env assertions, ENOENT detection, non-zero exit detection; enhanced mock with `mockChild()` helper |
| `systemd-unit.ts` | Add `ExecStartPre` marker guard, `RestartPreventExitStatus=80`; keep `KillMode=control-group` with updated comment |
| `systemd-unit.test.ts` | Updated KillMode assertion to verify `control-group` |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run --project gateway-methods src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts
 ✓ gateway-methods (8 tests) 632ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

$ npx vitest run --project unit-fast src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts
 ✓ unit-fast (4 tests) 2ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: systemd `KillMode=control-group` kills detached update handoff helper on gateway restart, preventing self-update.
- **Real environment tested**: OrbStack Linux VM (Ubuntu 22.04 Jammy, kernel 6.19.13-orbstack, aarch64) with full user-level systemd and `loginctl enable-linger`. Node.js v24.15.0.

### Live systemd proof: `--scope` escapes cgroup, helper survives service restart

Created a systemd user service with `KillMode=control-group` (matching OpenClaw's generated unit at `src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts:86`):

```ini
[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/tmp/handoff-test/gateway-sim.sh
KillMode=control-group
Restart=no
```

**Test A: WITH `systemd-run --user --scope` (this PR)**

```
=== Gateway running ===
     Active: active (running) since Wed 2026-05-20 20:08:58 CST
   Main PID: 2797 (gateway-sim.sh)
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/openclaw-gateway-test.service

=== Helper started (in separate scope unit, sleeping 8s) ===
Helper PID=2799 started in scope at Wed May 20 08:08:58 PM CST 2026
Helper: sleeping 8s while gateway lives...

=== KILLING GATEWAY (KillMode=control-group) at 20:09:00 ===
Gateway service stopped

=== Immediately after kill ===
Helper still running (no sentinel yet — helper still working)

=== After 10s wait (helper finished at 20:09:06) ===
Helper: still alive after 8s! Gateway should be dead by now
Helper: writing sentinel at Wed May 20 08:09:06 PM CST 2026
Sentinel: update-complete
```

**Result**: Helper survived gateway service kill and completed work ✅

**Test B: WITHOUT `systemd-run --scope` (current main behavior)**

Same setup but using plain `bash &` with `disown` (simulating current detached spawn):

```
=== Helper started (no scope, sleeping 8s) ===
Helper PID=2844 (NO SCOPE) started at Wed May 20 08:09:44 PM CST 2026
Helper: sleeping 8s...

=== KILLING GATEWAY (KillMode=control-group) at 20:09:46 ===

=== After 10s wait ===
Helper log: stopped at "sleeping 8s..." — no further output
Sentinel: FAILED: helper was KILLED by KillMode=control-group!
```

**Result**: Helper was killed when gateway service stopped ❌

### Comparison

| Scenario | Helper survives service stop? | Sentinel written? |
|----------|------------------------------|-------------------|
| `systemd-run --user --scope` (this PR) | ✅ Yes — ran in separate scope unit | ✅ `update-complete` |
| Plain detached child (current main) | ❌ No — killed by `KillMode=control-group` | ❌ Never created |

### Security: env via spawn(), not argv

```typescript
// env passed to spawn() for systemd-run — not in command-line args
child = spawn("systemd-run", [...], { cwd: params.root, env, detached: true, stdio: "ignore" });
```

Test coverage verifies explicitly:
```typescript
expect(options.env).toBeDefined();
expect(args).not.toContain("--setenv");
```

### What was not tested

- Full end-to-end `openclaw update` flow (requires published package version delta and npm registry)
- Live `ExecStartPre` marker file guard (defense-in-depth only, verified by code inspection)
- `--collect` garbage collection timing (non-functional, only prevents stale unit name conflicts)

Closes #84068
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

```
Sentinel: update-complete
```

**Result**: Helper survived gateway service kill and completed work ✅

**Test B: WITHOUT `systemd-run --scope` (current main behavior)**

Same setup but using plain `bash &` with `disown` (simulating current detached spawn):

```
=== Helper started (no scope, sleeping 8s) ===
Helper PID=2844 (NO SCOPE) started at Wed May 20 08:09:44 PM CST 2026
Helper: sleeping 8s...

=== KILLING GATEWAY (KillMode=control-group) at 20:09:46 ===

=== After 10s wait ===
Helper log: stopped at "sleeping 8s..." — no further output
Sentinel: FAILED: helper was KILLED by KillMode=control-group!
```

**Result**: Helper was killed when gateway service stopped ❌

### Comparison

| Scenario | Helper survives service stop? | Sentinel written? |
|----------|------------------------------|-------------------|
| `systemd-run --user --scope` (this PR) | ✅ Yes — ran in separate scope unit | ✅ `update-complete` |
| Plain detached child (current main) | ❌ No — killed by `KillMode=control-group` | ❌ Never created |

### Security: env via spawn(), not argv

```typescript
// env passed to spawn() for systemd-run — not in command-line args
child = spawn("systemd-run", [...], { cwd: params.root, env, detached: true, stdio: "ignore" });
```

Test coverage verifies explicitly:
```typescript
expect(options.env).toBeDefined();
expect(args).not.toContain("--setenv");
```

### What was not tested

- Full end-to-end `openclaw update` flow (requires published package version delta and npm registry)
- Live `ExecStartPre` marker file guard (defense-in-depth only, verified by code inspection)
- `--collect` garbage collection timing (non-functional, only prevents stale unit name conflicts)

Closes #84068

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(daemon): escape systemd cgroup for update handoff via systemd-run --scope
target.body:
## Summary

On systemd-supervised installs, clicking "Update now" silently fails: the update handoff helper is created but immediately killed by systemd's `KillMode=control-group` when the gateway unit restarts. The helper never executes, the version stays unchanged, and the UI shows "Update skipped: managed-service-handoff-started."

This PR detects systemd supervision and launches the handoff helper via `systemd-run --user --scope` to escape the gateway's cgroup. Non-systemd supervisors (launchd, etc.) continue using the existing detached-child path.

## Root cause

systemd's `KillMode=control-group` (the default) kills all processes in the service's cgroup on restart — including detached child processes. The handoff helper, despite being `unref()`'d and spawned with `detached: true`, stays in the same cgroup and is terminated before it can run `npm install`.

## Fix

```
systemd-run --user --scope --unit openclaw-update-<handoffId> --collect -- node handoff.cjs handoff.json
```

Key design choices:

**`--scope` not `--service`**: `--scope` inherits the caller's environment (profile, state dir, config, PATH, proxy, package manager discovery all preserved). `--service` runs in a clean systemd environment and would lose all service env vars.

**`KillMode=control-group` preserved**: The scoped update helper escapes via `systemd-run --scope` into its own cgroup, so `KillMode=control-group` safely stops the gateway and all its child workers without affecting the handoff. No change to existing shutdown semantics.

**Launch failure detection**: After spawning `systemd-run`, the code awaits its exit code with a 5s timeout. If systemd-run exits non-zero (user session unavailable, D-Bus down, etc.), an error is thrown before reporting "handoff started" — preventing the gateway from exiting 80 and leaving the service stale.

**No secret exposure via argv**: The `env` object is passed via `spawn()` option — identical to the existing non-systemd path. No `--setenv=KEY=*** args are used. Secrets stay in `/proc/PID/environ` (restricted to same UID), not in `/proc/PID/cmdline` (world-readable).

Additional hardening:
- `sanitizeSystemdUnitName()` strips non-`[a-zA-Z0-9:._-]` chars from `handoffId`
- `/tmp/openclaw-update-in-progress` marker file for `ExecStartPre` defense-in-depth guard
- `--collect` flag ensures scope units are garbage-collected after exit

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `update-managed-service-handoff.ts` | Detect `supervisor === "systemd"` → spawn via `systemd-run --user --scope`; await exit code to detect launch failures; add marker file lifecycle; add `sanitizeSystemdUnitName()` |
| `update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts` | 3 new tests: systemd `--scope` spawn path with env assertions, ENOENT detection, non-zero exit detection; enhanced mock with `mockChild()` helper |
| `systemd-unit.ts` | Add `ExecStartPre` marker guard, `RestartPreventExitStatus=80`; keep `KillMode=control-group` with updated comment |
| `systemd-unit.test.ts` | Updated KillMode assertion to verify `control-group` |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run --project gateway-methods src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts
 ✓ gateway-methods (8 tests) 632ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

$ npx vitest run --project unit-fast src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts
 ✓ unit-fast (4 tests) 2ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: systemd `KillMode=control-group` kills detached update handoff helper on gateway restart, preventing self-update.
- **Real environment tested**: OrbStack Linux VM (Ubuntu 22.04 Jammy, kernel 6.19.13-orbstack, aarch64) with full user-level systemd and `loginctl enable-linger`. Node.js v24.15.0.

### Live systemd proof: `--scope` escapes cgroup, helper survives service restart

Created a systemd user service with `KillMode=control-group` (matching OpenClaw's generated unit at `src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts:86`):

```ini
[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/tmp/handoff-test/gateway-sim.sh
KillMode=control-group
Restart=no
```

**Test A: WITH `systemd-run --user --scope` (this PR)**

```
=== Gateway running ===
     Active: active (running) since Wed 2026-05-20 20:08:58 CST
   Main PID: 2797 (gateway-sim.sh)
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/openclaw-gateway-test.service

=== Helper started (in separate scope unit, sleeping 8s) ===
Helper PID=2799 started in scope at Wed May 20 08:08:58 PM CST 2026
Helper: sleeping 8s while gateway lives...

=== KILLING GATEWAY (KillMode=control-group) at 20:09:00 ===
Gateway service stopped

=== Immediately after kill ===
Helper still running (no sentinel yet — helper still working)

=== After 10s wait (helper finished at 20:09:06) ===
Helper: still alive after 8s! Gateway should be dead by now
Helper: writing sentinel at Wed May 20 08:09:06 PM CST 2026
Sentinel: update-complete
```

**Result**: Helper survived gateway service kill and completed work ✅

**Test B: WITHOUT `systemd-run --scope` (current main behavior)**

Same setup but using plain `bash &` with `disown` (simulating current detached spawn):

```
=== Helper started (no scope, sleeping 8s) ===
Helper PID=2844 (NO SCOPE) started at Wed May 20 08:09:44 PM CST 2026
Helper: sleeping 8s...

=== KILLING GATEWAY (KillMode=control-group) at 20:09:46 ===

=== After 10s wait ===
Helper log: stopped at "sleeping 8s..." — no further output
Sentinel: FAILED: helper was KILLED by KillMode=control-group!
```

**Result**: Helper was killed when gateway service stopped ❌

### Comparison

| Scenario | Helper survives service stop? | Sentinel written? |
|----------|------------------------------|-------------------|
| `systemd-run --user --scope` (this PR) | ✅ Yes — ran in separate scope unit | ✅ `update-complete` |
| Plain detached child (current main) | ❌ No — killed by `KillMode=control-group` | ❌ Never created |

### Security: env via spawn(), not argv

```typescript
// env passed to spawn() for systemd-run — not in command-line args
child = spawn("systemd-run", [...], { cwd: params.root, env, detached: true, stdio: "ignore" });
```

Test coverage verifies explicitly:
```typescript
expect(options.env).toBeDefined();
expect(args).not.toContain("--setenv");
```

### What was not tested

- Full end-to-end `openclaw update` flow (requires published package version delta and npm registry)
- Live `ExecStartPre` marker file guard (defense-in-depth only, verified by code inspection)
- `--collect` garbage collection timing (non-functional, only prevents stale unit name conflicts)

Closes #84068
